questions.question: Compare the transmission distances of single-mode and multi-mode fiber optic cables.
keyPoints: Single-mode fiber: up to 100km without repeaters (typically used for WANs/long haul)
Multi-mode fiber: up to 2km for 100Mbps, 550m for 1Gbps (typical LAN applications)
Distance difference due to modal dispersion in multi-mode fiber

questions.question: Explain the difference between UTP and STP cables and state where each is used.
keyPoints: UTP (Unshielded Twisted Pair): No metallic shielding, cheaper, susceptible to EMI
STP (Shielded Twisted Pair): Has metallic shielding, better EMI protection, more expensive
UTP used in most LANs, STP used in environments with high EMI like factories

questions.question: What is crosstalk in twisted pair cables? How can it be minimized?
keyPoints: Crosstalk is interference between adjacent wire pairs in twisted pair cables
Types: NEXT (Near-End Crosstalk) and FEXT (Far-End Crosstalk)
Minimization methods: twisting wire pairs, using shielding (STP), using higher category cables

questions.question: List four advantages of fiber optic cables over copper twisted pair cables.
keyPoints: Much higher bandwidth capacity (Gbps vs Mbps)
Lower signal attenuation over long distances
Immunity to electromagnetic interference
Better security - difficult to tap without detection
Lighter weight and smaller diameter
No electrical conduction - safe in hazardous environments

questions.question: What is the significance of the 100-meter limit for twisted pair Ethernet cables?
keyPoints: 100 meters is the maximum distance from NIC to hub/switch for 10/100/1000BASE-T
Beyond this distance, signal attenuation degrades data integrity
This limit ensures reliable signal transmission without repeaters
The limit includes patch cables, so permanent links are typically 90 meters

questions.question: Differentiate between 10BASE2 and 10BASE5 Ethernet standards.
keyPoints: 10BASE2 (Thinnet): Coaxial cable, max segment 185m, uses BNC connectors, 10 Mbps
10BASE5 (Thicknet): Thicker coaxial cable, max segment 500m, uses AUI connectors, 10 Mbps
Thicknet has better shielding and longer reach but more expensive and harder to install

questions.question: Explain the concept of attenuation in guided media.
keyPoints: Attenuation is the gradual loss of signal strength as it travels through the medium
Measured in decibels (dB) per unit distance
Caused by resistance in copper cables and scattering/absorption in fiber optics
Higher frequencies and longer distances result in greater attenuation
Repeaters are used to amplify and regenerate signals

questions.question: Describe the three main types of guided media used in computer networks. Discuss their construction, characteristics, advantages, disadvantages, and typical applications.
keyPoints: Twisted Pair: Construction (4 pairs twisted, UTP/STP), characteristics (Cat3-8, 10M-40G), advantages (cheap, easy), disadvantages (EMI, limited distance), applications (LANs, telephone)

Coaxial Cable: Construction (core, insulation, shield, jacket), characteristics (50-75 ohm, Thinnet/Thicknet), advantages (better shielding, longer), disadvantages (expensive, bulky), applications (cable TV, legacy Ethernet)

Fiber Optic: Construction (core, cladding, jacket), characteristics (single/multi-mode, THz bandwidth), advantages (high speed, long distance, immune to EMI), disadvantages (expensive, special tools), applications (backbones, WANs, data centers)

questions.question: Explain the EIA/TIA category standards for twisted pair cables. Also describe the factors that affect the performance of twisted pair cables.
keyPoints: Categories: Cat3 (10Mbps), Cat5 (100Mbps), Cat5e (1Gbps), Cat6 (1/10Gbps), Cat6a (10Gbps), Cat7 (10Gbps), Cat8 (25-40Gbps)
Factors: Attenuation (signal loss), Crosstalk (NEXT/FEXT), Impedance (100 ohm for Ethernet), Noise/EMI susceptibility, Cable length, Installation quality (proper termination, bend radius)

questions.question: Compare single-mode and multi-mode fiber optic cables in terms of core diameter, light source, bandwidth, distance, and applications.
keyPoints: Core Diameter: Single-mode (8-10μm), Multi-mode (50-62.5μm)
Light Source: Single-mode (laser), Multi-mode (LED/VCSEL)
Bandwidth: Single-mode (higher THz), Multi-mode (lower GHz)
Distance: Single-mode (100km+), Multi-mode (550m-2km)
Applications: Single-mode (telecom, long-haul), Multi-mode (LANs, data centers)
Cost: Single-mode (more expensive), Multi-mode (less expensive)

questions.question: Discuss the various connector types used in fiber optic networks. Explain the purpose of repeaters in guided media networks.
keyPoints: Connector Types: ST (bayonet, round), SC (push-pull, square), LC (small form factor), MT-RJ (mechanical transfer)
Repeater Purpose: Amplify and regenerate weakened signals, extend network segment length, overcome attenuation, allow longer cable runs between devices
